title: His Charge To Julia At His Death.
content:
Dearest of thousands, now the time draws near
    That with my lines my life must full-stop here.
    Cut off thy hairs, and let thy tears be shed
    Over my turf when I am buried.
    Then for effusions, let none wanting be,
    Or other rites that do belong to me;
    As love shall help thee, when thou dost go hence
    Unto thy everlasting residence.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "His Charge To Julia At His Death.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
